patois first brought dominica seventeenth eighteenth century slaves arrived french settlers martinique guadeloupe , french colonies established .
dominica sandwiched islands , miles south guade loupe miles north martinique .
early french settlers mainly woodcutters later became small farmers , cultivating mainly tobacco cotton .
coffee introduced early eighteenth century , soon became important crop , earning much wealth growers .
though denied political power coming british , french planters remained numerically economically strong well nineteenth century , even , many estates taken mulattos martinique guadeloupe ( honeychurch : ) .
british assumed control , slaves belonging french ran away formed maroon settlements mountains , soon joined newly arrived slaves , among others .
settlements became patois-speaking enclaves .
time , plantations , slaves stayed remained openly loyal french owners .
patois became symbol 'frenchness ' .
patois language acquired majority dominicans , approximately percent african descent .
significant ethnic minority made descendants caribs controlled island coming europeans .
centuries , caribs gradually abandoned language patois .
linguistically longer distinguishable group .
ethnic separateness , , threatened , since majority labeled mixed ancestry .
patois remains structurally close vernaculars neighbouring french islands st. lucia .
despite differences , many relate fact patois coexists english , whereas french official language martinique guadeloupe , language provides bond islands .
although st. lucia , like dominica , 'an glophone ' , slightly away french islands dominica direct air links , example .

planktonic communities along south coast studied flooding .
flood waters kingston harbour flowed southwest along hellshire coast far wreck reef , confined within km shore .
water bays along lower hellshire coast remained clear unaffected harbour water , although planktonic communities showed marked changes .
port royal cays area much shelf unaffected .

order establish levels similarity communities stations , two indices cal culated entire plankton community phytoplankton genera zooplankton taxon data .
: jaccard community coefficient ( jcc ) ( clifford & stephenson ) percent simi larity coefficient ( psc ) ( whittaker & fairbanks , lee & mcalice ) .
jcc neglects conjoint absences based presence absence , therefore crediting single rare occurrences much weight occurrences numerous regular ; contrast , psc neglects single rare occur rences .

earlier systems red mud disposal red mud slurry came direct last stage mud washing thickeners alumina plant , conse quently fairly fluid slurry .
reaching `` mud pond '' slurry would separate quickly two phases  liquid phase , dilute solution caustic liquor circulating alumina plant , semi-solid phase , much viscous slurry coming plant .

improvement mud disposal system described so-called `` dry stacking '' method originated germany described schepers haerter .
recently pohland tielens reviewed method .
adopted several alumina plants s. similar method mineral tailings first reported robinsky .
, essence method red mud slurry would dewatered ( i.e .
solids concentration increased ) suf ficiently leaving alumina plant would immediately separate two phases .
usually done vacuum filtering washed mud slurry plant .
produced filter `` cake '' pumped mud ponds high pressure pumps ( usually cake liquified violent agitation addition small amount water pumped ) .
besides less liquid lose ( thereby reducing pollution risk pond sealed ) , dewatering thickening red mud slurry ex tent high pressure pumps needed transport gives additional advantage flow readily .
therefore piles form low angle `` stack '' gradient , comes rest , % % ( units verti cally units horizontally ) depending `` thick '' mud slurry made .
ad vantage stack angle rain water runs readily stack rather re-wetting mud happens earlier method .
though takes long time , red mud stacks dry become hard , whereas mud ponds never dry .

text introduction computer models increasingly applied management irrigation schemes main systems level .
inca model ( bird & makin , ) facilitates processing scheme data graphical database interface camsis model ( burton , ) allows user choose several water allocation policies .

background rio cobre scheme rio cobre irrigation scheme located southern st catherine , jamaica command area ha .
water diverted rio cobre river canal network distribution users growing variety crops .
annual rainfall irrigated areas averages less mm , % falls months september october .
figure shows actual rainfall average rainfall innswood station within rio cobre scheme .
average class pan evaporation mm year - peak mm day - july .
discharge river water diverted system generally inadequate meet requirements crops months growing season , even wet year .
scheme grows variety crops including sugarcane , pasture , vegetables freshwater fish .
sugarcane far predominant crop accounting % cultivated area .

diversion structure river breached may , resulting capacity reduced - .

picture caption diagram caption irmos model irmos model tool computer-aided management optimal water allocation irrigation system .
model two main modules , one mainly data management water management scheduling .
model designed allow optimal water allocation main systems level schemes one sources water , several tertiary quaternary units complex cropping patterns .
data management component model involves processing infor mation describes physical characteristics scheme , climate history , seasonal information periodic data .
crop coefficient root depth different development stages , yield response information , production costs unit price stored database crop .
seasonal data describe season , period length , planned cropping pattern , expected water supply information required preseason planning .
periodic data include planting , harvesting , water application , rainfall evaporation .
schematic layout rio cobre scheme ( fig . )
typical scheme modelled irmos .
scheme typically comprise main secondary canal network supplies water off-takes water management units ( wmu ) .
wmu may contain tertiary canals , farms fields .
purpose model wmu contains number basic analytical units called plot .
plot unique soil type cropping pattern , point time plot 's crop coefficient , yield sensitivity factor , root depth status determined .
water management module uses information database perform analyses necessary planning , operating performance assessment irrigation system .
normal run planning operation , irmos generally estimates available water supply , determines plot crop water requirement expected input rainfall .
proceeds allocate available water according user selected water allocation policy .
two water allocation policies available irmos performance orientated .
one allocates water minimize crop losses period maximize net benefits planning horizon .
performance assessment involves evaluation various performance indicators derived data actual history scheme specified number consecutive operational periods .
also allows hindsight analyses enabling user assess scheme would performed operated differently , e.g .
rainfall probability water allocation policy different .
performance indicators following definitions performance indicators allows irmos track performance irrigation scheme : unsatisfied demand ratio ( udr ) udr defined instance water allocated less demand ratio shortfall demand .
one instance water deficit udr computed ratio sum deficits sum demands .
over-supply ratio ( osr ) osr defined instance volume water allocated higher demand ratio volume allocated excess demand total volume supplied .
one instance water deficit osr computed ratio sum excess allocations sum allocations .
relative evapotranspiration relative evapotranspiration crop ratio actual evapotranspiration potential evapotranspiration prescribed period .
relative evapotranspiration unity indicates crop evaporated potential rate throughout period .
relative yield relative yield crop ratio actual yield maximum yield expected growing conditions .
doorenbos & kassam ( ) proposed following linear relationship relative yield relative evapo transpiration given growth period : yr = - ky ( - er ) yr relative yield , ky yield sensitivity index growth period , er relative evapotranspiration .
specific yield specific yield defined crop production per unit volume water .
irmos model specific yield water management unit computed sum values crops harvested within unit per million cubic metres water allocated unit .

brown pelican brown pelican old joe called lo cally , seen often live sea ; sometimes fly inland feed fish farms reservoirs .
big bird , inches ( cm ) length , stocky , squat body , short tail legs ; wings broad rounded .
bill long hooked tip large pouch skin gives bird unusual appearance .
pelicans often seen flying low water , one , flapping wings slowly heads drawn back shoulders .
brownish-grey colour , breeding season plumage becomes much richer brown .
adults white streak running top head sides neck .
area around eye featherless .

scoop fish large , pouched bill surface toss fish swallow .
pelicans make untidy nests low bushes ground , laying two three white eggs .

natural history termites jamaica pamela clarke , department zoology , uwi , mona photograph introduction termites distributed mainly tropics species inhabiting forests structures associated human habitation .
chronic association inspired numerous studies biology control termites .
intensive studies jamaica 's termites commenced university west indies .
termites ( order isoptera ) consist five families : mastotermitidae , hodotermitidae , rhinotermitidae , kalotermitidae termitidae .
first four families collectively termed lower termites , species family termitidae referred higher advanced termites .
termites jamaica termites commonly referred white ants jamaica , colloquially called duck ants chi-chi .
erroneous classification termites ants arises superficial resemblance .
two simple diagnostic features may used distinguish termites ) soft generally white bodies ) absence `` waist '' ( constriction thorax abdomen ) .
seventeen species termites jamaica ( see box ) .
contrast many animal groups , none species endemic island also occur regions .
jamaica 's termites represent three five families : kalotermitidae collectively called dry wood termites .
name implies , inhabit wood provides shelter food .
species termites well-known man voraciously consume domestic commercial items containing cellulose ( eg .
furniture wooden frames buildings ) .
c. brevis common pest building timber .
ingested wood particles digested flagellated protozoa ( specific lower termites ancestors ) .
excrement frass released small , dry pellets , filters holes wood .
pellets used indicators presence drywood termites .
rhinotermitidae consist popular subterranean damp-wood termites .
subterranean termites build nests surface ground radiate search food .
rarely seen , presence confirmed trails provide indispensable link moist underground .
often complicated nests many different parts house large populations .
parent nest original nest containing reproductives .
subsidiary nests constructed completion parent nest usually designated various functions brood chambers food reserves .
multi- regional nests specialized sections offers many escape routes enhance resilience subterranean termites insecticides .
however , interaction nests facilitates spread introduced toxic substances .
termitidae include arboreal termites also construct large , complex multi-regional nests intricately designed walls .
nests restricted trees built walls roofs buildings .
nests grow large sizes accommodate continuously increasing colony .
common species n. nigriceps , found diverse habitats jamaica .
behaviour social system family termitidae complex highly organized lower termites ; consequently , capable efficient foraging , defence nests regulation colony size .
social caste system termites exhibit highest form sociality known animals ; system ( termed eusociality ) characterized presence non-reproductive caste ( `` workers '' , `` soldiers '' specialists ) , overlapping adult generations cooperative brood care .
non- reproductive individuals develop fertilized eggs ; different social hymenopterans ( bees , ants , social wasps ) workers develop unfertilised eggs .
non-reproductive castes forgo reproduction ; instead stay parent nest help mother father rear additional brothers sisters .
known reproductive altruism .
nine different castes identified termite colonies , full complement never occur simultaneously single nest .
immature termites separated two group depending upon presence wings apterous ( wingless ) brachypterous nymphs ; two forms also called larvae nymphs , respectively .
remaining castes referred adults potential development subdivided sterile reproductive individuals .
groups morphologically physiologically adapted specific functions .
super-specialization castes enables greater efficiency execution jobs ultimately enhances success colonies .

reserved book collection ( rbc ) rbc ?
rbc collection reserved books recommended lecturers undergraduate reading lists .
purpose rbc ?
rbc aims provide necessary reading material large numbers students classes various sizes .
loan period shorter normal allow access greatest number students .
loan period ?
usually overnight , reading room , three hour loan .
enough books student course ?
multiple copies purchased courses large numbers students , items serve one course .
however , copies often enough satisfy demand .
therefore quickly items returned , quickly become available others .
borrow rbc registered students university also registered library .
issuing system work ?
rbc operates first come , first served basis numbering system used issuing items .
failure obtain item might mean copies issued already .
might also mean item never recommended lecturer , therefore rbc .
help ?
make sure item need rbc checking catalogue .
fill reading room/overnight loan forms correctly .
uwi id card well library registration card times .
return items promptly many students possible access items .
penalties late return books ?
yes .
fines charged overdue books hour hour loan reading room books day overnight books .
please check circulation desk current charges .
